title: New MPE Formation in End of Evangelion
display_title: New MPE Formation in End of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: New MPE Formation in End of Evangelion             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Aug 24, 2012 6:26 pm The MP Evangelions take on a few different formations within End of Evangelion, from a simple circle to the Tree of Life to a multi-faceted infinity symbol. As I was watching the movie a while ago, I noticed a formation that I hadn't seen before. It's seen at time 1:06:37 in the movie, while the Geofront is rising from the earth along with the MP Evangelions and Unit-01. It's only visible for a few frames, so it doesn't surprise me that I've missed it for so long. Here it is: Can anyone identify it? Also, can someone tell me what script I should use to post images with that scroll thing on the bottom? I'm kinda new to this...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 25, 2012 12:31 am It's a variation on the Triquestra .

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Aug 25, 2012 7:46 am Reichu wrote: It's a variation on the Triquestra .

[]:             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Sat Nov 07, 2015 11:21 am Specifically, it's an inverted form of the triquerta, could be symbolic of an inverted trinity of sorts whereas the Christian tradition has the Father, The Mother, Holy Ghost, this could be a feminized version representing either the three Reis OR it could be Kaworu, Rei, and Yui (or both).

Article: New MPE Formation in End of Evangelion
[]: New MPE Formation in End of Evangelion             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Aug 24, 2012 6:26 pm The MP Evangelions take on a few different formations within End of Evangelion, from a simple circle to the Tree of Life to a multi-faceted infinity symbol. As I was watching the movie a while ago, I noticed a formation that I hadn't seen before. It's seen at time 1:06:37 in the movie, while the Geofront is rising from the earth along with the MP Evangelions and Unit-01. It's only visible for a few frames, so it doesn't surprise me that I've missed it for so long. Here it is: Can anyone identify it? Also, can someone tell me what script I should use to post images with that scroll thing on the bottom? I'm kinda new to this...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 25, 2012 12:31 am It's a variation on the Triquestra .

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Aug 25, 2012 7:46 am Reichu wrote: It's a variation on the Triquestra .

[]:             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Sat Nov 07, 2015 11:21 am Specifically, it's an inverted form of the triquerta, could be symbolic of an inverted trinity of sorts whereas the Christian tradition has the Father, The Mother, Holy Ghost, this could be a feminized version representing either the three Reis OR it could be Kaworu, Rei, and Yui (or both).
